summary: Complete testing of Login Info page PIN management features. All backend API tests passed (14/14). All frontend features verified with Playwright.
critical_code_review_comments: Login Info page: All three levels (Low, Medium, High) are locked by default - VERIFIED
Login Info page: Unlock button appears for all three levels - VERIFIED
Login Info page: Low level unlock with PIN '1111' works - VERIFIED
Login Info page: Medium level unlock with PIN '2222' works - VERIFIED
Login Info page: High level unlock with PIN '3401' works - VERIFIED
Login Info page: Wrong PIN is rejected for all levels with 'Incorrect PIN' toast - VERIFIED
Login Info page: PIN Settings button exists - VERIFIED
Login Info page: PIN Settings dialog first asks for admin PIN (3401) - VERIFIED
Login Info page: Wrong admin PIN in PIN Settings shows 'Incorrect admin PIN' toast - VERIFIED
Login Info page: After admin verification, can set Low and Medium PINs - VERIFIED
Login Info page: Only level_1 and level_2 options available in PIN Settings level dropdown - VERIFIED
Login Info page: Shows PIN status (Low PIN: Set, Medium PIN: Set, High PIN: Always set (admin)) - VERIFIED
Backend: GET /api/pins/status returns level_1_pin_set field - VERIFIED
Backend: POST /api/pins/set rejects level_3 pin_type with 400 error - VERIFIED
Backend: POST /api/pins/verify works for level_1 with stored PIN - VERIFIED
Door Codes page: Still works with hard PIN 3401 (no regression) - VERIFIED
passed_tests: GET /api/pins/status returns all required fields (shared_pin_set, level_1_pin_set, level_2_pin_set, level_3_pin_set)
POST /api/pins/verify with pin=3401 and pin_type=level_3 returns {valid:true}
POST /api/pins/verify with pin=1234 and pin_type=level_3 returns {valid:false}
POST /api/pins/verify with pin=3401 and pin_type=shared returns {valid:true}
POST /api/pins/verify with pin=wrong and pin_type=shared returns {valid:false}
POST /api/pins/verify with pin=1111 and pin_type=level_1 returns {valid:true}
POST /api/pins/verify with pin=9999 and pin_type=level_1 returns {valid:false}
POST /api/pins/verify with pin=2222 and pin_type=level_2 returns {valid:true}
POST /api/pins/set with correct admin_pin=3401 for level_1 succeeds
POST /api/pins/set with correct admin_pin=3401 for level_2 succeeds
POST /api/pins/set with wrong admin_pin returns 403 'Invalid admin PIN'
POST /api/pins/set for level_3 returns 400 'Can only set PINs for level_1 and level_2'
POST /api/pins/set for shared returns 400
PIN change persists in database
Login Info page loads with all three levels locked
Login Info page PIN Settings button visible
Login Info page Level 1 unlocks with PIN 1111
Login Info page Level 2 unlocks with PIN 2222
Login Info page Level 3 unlocks with PIN 3401
Login Info page wrong PIN rejected with toast
Login Info page PIN Settings requires admin PIN 3401
Login Info page PIN Settings wrong admin PIN rejected
Login Info page PIN Settings level dropdown shows only Low and Medium
Door Codes page admin code unlock with PIN 3401 works
seed_data_creation: Level 1 PIN set to 1111, Level 2 PIN set to 2222 via API during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: PIN management system: level_1 (Low) and level_2 (Medium) PINs are stored in DB settings collection. level_3 (High) and shared PINs are hard-coded to 3401 in backend (HARD_PIN constant in /app/backend/routers/admin.py). Login Info page has PIN Settings button that requires admin PIN (3401) to access. PIN Settings dialog has two steps: 1) verify admin PIN, 2) select level (Low/Medium only) and set new PIN. All three sensitivity levels are locked by default on Login Info page. Door Codes page uses 'shared' pin_type which verifies against hard-coded 3401. Key data-testids: login-info-page-title, login-info-pin-settings, login-unlock-level-1/2/3, login-pin-input, login-pin-verify-btn, login-pin-admin-verify-input, login-pin-admin-verify-btn, login-pin-level-select, login-pin-setup-input, login-pin-setup-save-btn. Current PINs: level_1=1111, level_2=2222, level_3/shared=3401. App password: emergeontop.
